channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Turns out the leak was accurate - Metal Gear Solid Delta: Snake Eater releases in August this year and the inner PS2 gamer in me cannot wait
item.description: Metal Gear Solid Delta: Snake Eater has its release date confirmed with a new trailer in February's PlayStation State of Play presentation.
item.dc:creator: rob.dwiar@futurenet.com (Rob Dwiar), TechRadar

item.title: Does the Nvidia RTX 5090 have a cable melting problem? It's complicated - but you probably don’t need to panic
item.description: Remember the Nvidia RTX 4090 cable-melting crisis? Claims that it’s back for the RTX 5090 might just be a load of hot air.
item.dc:creator: christian.guyton@futurenet.com (Christian Guyton), TechRadar

item.title: Your YouTube TV app could get much easier to use with these 2 new features
item.description: The YouTube TV app is set to get some key changes, with YouTube CEO Neal Mohan laying out the platform’s plans for 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple event rumors – here’s when we could see the iPhone SE 4, MacBook Air M4 and more
item.description: The Apple rumors are flying ahead of possible launches for the iPhone SE 4 and M4 MacBook Air –here's when they could land and how likely we think an Apple Spring event is.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: What is SASE? How the cloud marries networking and security
item.description: Secure Access Service Edge (SASE) is a network architecture that combines software-defined wide area networking (SD-WAN) and security functionality into a unified cloud service that promises simplified WAN deployments, improved efficiency and security, and application-specific bandwidth policies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Italian Government Denies Spying on Journalists with Spyware - 2024
item.description: The Italian government has denied using spyware to monitor journalists and migrant activists, despite reports that at least seven Italian cellphones were hacked with military-grade surveillance technology.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rising Competition in AI: OpenAI Shifts Strategy with GPT-5 Integration
item.description: OpenAI is rethinking its AI model strategy, integrating 'o3' into GPT-5 and signaling a shift toward fewer, more controlled models amid rising competition and cost pressures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meet the Bop House, the internet’s divisive new OnlyFans hype house
item.description: What if the Playboy Mansion was filled with OnlyFans content creators? That’s the pitch for the Bop House, a TikTok page that has gained nearly three million followers since its launch in December.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s SBM publishes blueprint for AI success
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s Vision 2030 emphasizes a technology-driven transformation to achieve its social, economic, and environmental goals, focusing on adopting and producing advanced innovations to position itself as a global leader in technological disruption.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Following Google, Apple’s maps now show Trump’s Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Apple renamed the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America on its maps Tuesday after an order by President Donald Trump was made official by the U.S. Geographic Names Information System.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: ChatGPT’s latest feature is a transformative leap for web research
item.description: I’m not sure who first compared ChatGPT to Cliff Clavin, the garrulous mailman/barfly from TV’s Cheers. It’s so an apt comparison that many people probably came up with it independently. Cliff did know a lot of stuff—after all, he was a Jeopardy almost-winner.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Tech security 101: How to spot a hidden tracker on your car
item.description: 80% of stalking victims are tracked using tech. GPS trackers, like AirTags, are among the most popular ways to do it. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: US Judge Rules Against Ross Intelligence in AI Training Dispute
item.description: US judge rules against Ross Intelligence, a legal AI startup, for using Thomson Reuters' Westlaw platform without permission to train their AI. Implications for future cases involving copyrighted material and AI training.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to not fall in love with AI-powered romance scammers
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to protect yourself against artificial intelligence-fueled romance scams, which have become the most common type of fraud in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 reasons Microsoft needn’t fear DeepSeek
item.description: The release of the latest version of the Chinese genAI bot DeepSeek last month upended the tech world when its creators claimed it was built for only $6 million — far less than the hundreds of billions of dollars Microsoft, Google, OpenAI, Meta, and others have poured into genAI development.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Keep your code open to possibilities
item.description: I’ve been developing software for many years now. Decades. I’ve learned a lot along the way, and I dare to think that I’ve collected some wisdom about software development worth sharing with others. At some point, you think “Yeah, I’ve got a grip on this thing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: On average, state cyber leaders are leaving their positions sooner
item.description: The National Association of State CIOs and Deloitte outlined five calls to action in a recent survey of state chief information security officers. The post On average, state cyber leaders are leaving their positions sooner first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Congress gets set to deal with artificial intelligence in health care
item.description: "AI is going to help kick in some of that load that the scientists and researchers do at the front end to quickly get a diagnosis," said Deema Tarazi. The post Congress gets set to deal with artificial intelligence in health care first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government
item.description: "There are some very prominent ones that we see every day, things like at the TSA with facial recognition," said Valerie Wirtschafter. The post How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Top 20 apps tracking you every day
item.description: The apps collecting the most data about you are among the most widely used. Kurt the CyberGuy takes a look at 20 of these apps and how you can protect personal information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
